rulings.Case Title: BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY VS. BURT HAMROL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 15, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFF BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION DENIED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PROVIDE COMPETENT EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING THE PRECISE AMOUNT OF MONEY THAT IS OWED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON DEFENDANT'S CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN ITS ENTIRETY. AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF FRAUD OR THAT THE INDIVIDUALS KNEW OR SHOULD HAVE KNOWN THAT DEFENDANT WOULD BE HARMED. ALSO, THE FACT THAT BOREL DELAYED FORECLOSING AND ATTEMPTING TO WORK OUT NEW LOANS SHOWS THAT THIS WAS NOT A "LOAN TO OWN" SCAM. AS TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION, THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT HAMROL KNEW THAT THE CONTRACTS LACKED THE ACCOUNT WHEN HE SIGNED THEM. FURTHER, PAYMENT DUE NOTICES INFORMED DEFENDANT THAT INTEREST PAYMENTS WERE COMING OUT OF THE LOAN SO THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BARS THE THEORY OF RECOVERY OF FAILURE TO PROVIDE AN INTEREST ACCOUNT. AS TO THE THEORY THAT THERE WAS A BREACH OF AN ORAL CONTRACT THIS IS BARRED UNDER PETERSON DEV. CO. INC. V. TORREY PINES BANK (1991) 233 CAL.APP.3D 103, 115. ALSO HAMROL IS ATTEMPTING TO INTRODUCE NEW ISSUES THAT ARE NOT IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, AS EXPLAINED UNDER THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE IS NO EVIDENCE RE: FRAUDULENT INTENT. AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION, HAMROL DOES NOT CITE ANY ILLEGALITY AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT BOREL'S PRACTICE WAS NOT FAIR. AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION, HAMROL CANNOT PROVE JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE. THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT HAMROL KNEW GREENE HAD NO AUTHORITY TO UNILATERALLY EXTEND THE LOANS AND NEEDED TO GO BEFORE THE LOAN COMMITTEE. AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION, INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION, BUT A REMEDY. AS TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO FORECLOSE. HAMROL ADMITTED THAT BOREL DID NOT TAKE ANYTHING FROM HIM AND DOES NOT OWE HIM MONEY. AS TO T

rulings.Case Title: GREAT AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK, A et al VS. U.S. FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 15, 2011, line 14, PLAINTIFF GREAT AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Plaintiff Great American Ins. Co¿s (¿GA¿) Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 2nd cause of action is granted. Plaintiff has presented evidence that the Policy has combined single limits of $500K per occurrence and $500K in the aggregate. This has shifted the burden to the defendant. Defendant has not presented any admissible evidence that would raise a triable issue of fact and has raised an untimely request for additional discovery (deposing Gain and Jensen). Plaintiff GA¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 3rd cause of action is denied. Defendant raises triable issues of fact as to whether the funds paid to settle the City of Modesto action are still available to settle the Orangeburg action. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: GREAT AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK, A et al VS. U.S. FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 15, 2011, line 14, PLAINTIFF NATIONAL SURETY CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Plaintiff National Surety Corps.¿ (¿NSC¿) Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 2nd cause of action is granted. Plaintiff has presented evidence that the Policy has combined single limits of $500K per occurrence and $500K in the aggregate. Even as to the 2400 Coffee Road laundry establishment, Platiff NSC has shifted the burden to show that the dry cleaning operation portion of the facility was rated on a remuneration basis and, therefore, applied toward aggregate limits. This has shifted the burden to the defendant. Defendant has not presented any admissible evidence that would raise a triable issue of fact and has raised an untimely request for additional discovery (deposing Gain and Jensen). Plaintiff NSC¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 4th cause of action is denied. Defendant raises triable issues of fact as to whether the funds held in trust to settle the City of Modesto action (which includes the Orangeburg location) are still available to remediate pollution in the Orangeburg action. NSC¿s objection to the Guite Declaration is overruled. =(302/LMG)
